tier_justification: Tier 1: one work-unit (a shape guard added to post_evals::smoke_verify), no irreversible/outward surface (no publish, deploy, migration, data deletion, external send). Above tier 0 because no existing test covers the malformed-.evals shape at the smoke_verify gate — the goal state is not already covered by an existing verify-criterion.

evals.assert: smoke-verify REFUSES (nonzero exit) an artifact whose .evals is a scalar number, where the fix makes it fail-closed.
evals.cmd: cd /Users/harrison/Github/coderails/.worktrees/smoke-verify-guard && f=$(mktemp) && printf '%s' '{"tier":1,"evals":42}' > "$f" && ! bash scripts/post_evals.sh smoke-verify "$f" 2bd1bce >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo REFUSED-CORRECTLY; rc=$?; unlink "$f"; exit $rc
evals.negative_control: cd /Users/harrison/Github/coderails/.worktrees/smoke-verify-guard && f=$(mktemp) && printf '%s' '{"tier":1,"evals":42}' > "$f" && git show origin/main:scripts/post_evals.sh > /tmp/pe-main.sh && bash /tmp/pe-main.sh smoke-verify "$f" 2bd1bce >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo MAIN-ACCEPTS-IT-BUG; rc=$?; unlink "$f"; exit $rc
evals.expected: cmd exits 0 (fixed code refuses the scalar). negative_control: current origin/main ACCEPTS the malformed artifact (exits 0, printing MAIN-ACCEPTS-IT-BUG) — proving the fail-open is real and the check discriminates fix-from-main.

evals.assert: smoke-verify REFUSES an artifact whose .evals is a string, and an object — all non-array shapes fail closed.
evals.cmd: cd /Users/harrison/Github/coderails/.worktrees/smoke-verify-guard && for body in '{"tier":1,"evals":"notarray"}' '{"tier":1,"evals":{"E1":{"mode":"scripted"}}}'; do f=$(mktemp); printf '%s' "$body" > "$f"; if bash scripts/post_evals.sh smoke-verify "$f" 2bd1bce >/dev/null 2>&1; then unlink "$f"; echo "LEAK: $body accepted"; exit 1; fi; unlink "$f"; done; echo ALL-NON-ARRAY-REFUSED
evals.negative_control: cd /Users/harrison/Github/coderails/.worktrees/smoke-verify-guard && f=$(mktemp) && printf '%s' '{"tier":1,"evals":"notarray"}' > "$f" && git show origin/main:scripts/post_evals.sh > /tmp/pe-main2.sh && bash /tmp/pe-main2.sh smoke-verify "$f" 2bd1bce >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo MAIN-LEAKS-STRING; rc=$?; unlink "$f"; exit $rc
evals.expected: cmd exits 0 (fixed code refuses both string and object). negative_control: main accepts the string shape (exits 0), confirming the leak class.

evals.assert: REGRESSION GUARD: a VALID array containing only agent-run (non-scripted) evals still PASSES (empty indices is legitimately nothing to re-execute — must NOT be refused).
evals.cmd: cd /Users/harrison/Github/coderails/.worktrees/smoke-verify-guard && f=$(mktemp) && printf '%s' '{"tier":1,"evals":[{"id":"E1","mode":"agent-run","priority":"P0"}]}' > "$f" && bash scripts/post_evals.sh smoke-verify "$f" 2bd1bce >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo AGENT-RUN-ONLY-PASSES; rc=$?; unlink "$f"; exit $rc
evals.negative_control: cd /Users/harrison/Github/coderails/.worktrees/smoke-verify-guard && f=$(mktemp) && printf '%s' '{"tier":1,"evals":"notarray"}' > "$f" && bash scripts/post_evals.sh smoke-verify "$f" 2bd1bce >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo SHOULD-NOT-PASS; rc=$?; unlink "$f"; exit $rc
evals.expected: cmd exits 0 (agent-run-only array is accepted — the fix does NOT over-tighten onto empty-indices). negative_control: the malformed string is refused by the FIXED code, so this control exits nonzero (fails), proving the check distinguishes valid-empty from malformed.

evals.assert: A regression test exists in the test suite covering the malformed-.evals shape at smoke_verify, and the full post_evals test suite passes.
evals.cmd: cd /Users/harrison/Github/coderails/.worktrees/smoke-verify-guard && grep -rlE 'evals.*(42|notarray|non-array|not an array|array)' hooks/scripts/tests/post_evals.test.sh >/dev/null && bash hooks/scripts/tests/post_evals.test.sh >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo SUITE-GREEN-WITH-REGRESSION-TEST
evals.expected: cmd exits 0 (a malformed-.evals regression test is present AND the suite is green). negative_control exits nonzero (the bogus marker is absent).
